Anthropic’s Claude models are now generally available in $MSFT Foundry on Azure running on $NVDA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs.

This marks the first time Nvidia and Anthropic have entered a deep co-engineering partnership.

Here’s my full deep dive on why $RKLB acquired $IRDM: 

piped.video/jtOn_VqaiL0?si=Atj1…

I personally love $RKLB ~$8B acquisition of $IRDM because because its buying the network layer of space where recurring, high-margin service revenue lives.

Launch becomes the cost advantage, connectivity becomes the monetization engine and combining both under one roof makes the company look more like a vertically integrated space platform.

That is the same structural lesson SpaceX taught the industry with Starlink and Rocket Lab is now building its own version of that model through acquisition instead of spending the next decade assembling every piece organically from scratch.

$ASTS is up nearly 18% after reports that Japan is expected to select Rakuten & AST SpaceMobile for a ~$1B satellite-to-phone project.

The funding would support BlueBird satellites and network infrastructure that can also be used globally through AST’s federated constellation.
$ALAB just became the latest 5-bagger in the Family Portfolio  🙃

$ASTS just received another major component delivery to accelerate Block-2 BlueBird satellite production.

BlueBirds 11, 12, and 13 are next to ship with satellites in production through BlueBird 37 as AST SpaceMobile builds world’s first space-based cellular broadband network.

$AMZN and Anthropic reportedly renegotiated part of their deal to price Claude usage by tokens instead of compute hours starting next year.

Amazon is now looking at OpenAI and its own Nova models to help lower AI costs.
$AXON is currently a top-three position in my Family Portfolio.
The Fear & Greed Index is back in extreme fear.

$OUST is up nearly 20% as Agility Robotics prepares to go public through $CCXI giving the U.S. its first pure-play humanoid robotics company.

Agility’s Digit robot uses its LiDAR giving Ouster direct exposure to one of the most commercial-ready humanoid robotics platforms.

$MU is now a top 10 holding in the S&P 500 with a 1.9% weighting.

A memory company becoming one of the largest stocks in the S&P 500 while still trading at a fraction of the market multiple shows the market still doesn't fully believe these AI memory profits will last.
$RKLB up over 12% after acquiring $IRDM in an ~$8B deal to become a fully integrated space power delivering critical communications to millions of users worldwide.

$META is restricting engineers use of Claude Code & OpenAI Codex for internal AI model-building work.

Zuck is reportedly concerned rival model outputs could leak into its own training data & create distillation issues as Meta tries to reduce reliance on outside AI coding tools.
$IREN IS NOW PART OF THE RUSSELL 1000 INDEX

Here’s why $RKLB just spent ~$8B acquiring $IRDM.

Rocket Lab wants to own the network, spectrum and recurring revenue layer once satellites are in orbit which is why this deal is really about vertical integration as Neutron comes online.

Once Rocket Lab can launch Iridium satellites on Neutron then it internalizes a cost that used to flow to third-party launch providers making the deal way more accretive over time as Neutron comes online.

What I like is that Iridium also brings something Rocket Lab stack has lacked which is recurring, high-margin service revenue tied to global connectivity, direct-to-device and government communications.

The space economy is splitting into companies that can launch and refresh their own networks and companies that have to rent access to orbit. $SPCX already proved which side wins because if you own both launch and the network then you control the cost structure, refresh cycle and margin stack in a way third-party launch customers cannot match.

Launch gets you to orbit but connectivity is what you monetize once you're there and the companies that own both the launch layer and the network become the platforms of the space economy.

$PLTR and $NVDA are expanding their partnership to bring sovereign AI to U.S. government and critical infrastructure agencies.

Palantir gives mission-critical organizations the operating layer to securely deploy, customize and govern Nvidia Nemotron models on their own data.

$RKLB just agreed to acquire $IRDM in an ~$8B deal adding Iridium’s global satellite network, rare spectrum and 2.55M+ subscribers.

The deal would turn Rocket Lab into a more complete space infrastructure platform across launch, satellites, spectrum and communications.

What if they flipped the offsides rule from any part of your body in front of the defender is offsides to any part of your body behind the defender is not offsides?

(hockey uses a fixed blue line for offsides. The puck has to cross the blue line first. A player is not offsides if any part of his body is on or behind the blue line when the puck crosses. So what I suggested is the way hockey defines a player being offsides.)

I break down why traders should be careful chasing today's strong gap higher after last week's sharp pullback. 

While the market is short-term oversold and due for a bounce, the real test is whether the $QQQ can reclaim and hold above key moving averages or if this becomes another failed rally. 

Rather than assuming the correction is over, you should stay tactical, trim into strength, and wait for confirmation before adding significant exposure.

*

$QQQ sits near an important technical decision point. 

You should closely watch whether it can hold above roughly the $715 area and reclaim nearby moving averages. 

If buyers fail to maintain the opening strength, another rejection could keep the recent pullback intact. 

Don't chase the opening gap today.

*

The semiconductor sector $SOXX $SMH remains the market's biggest focus. 

I think $MU may now be entering a healthy consolidation phase following its massive rally. 

Instead of chasing the stock higher or aggressively shorting it, let it digest gains and rebuild its technical structure. 

$SNDK recent pullback simply brought it back toward its short-term moving average rather than signaling the end of its trend.

*

We captured a significant options gain in $SPCX after buying calls during Friday's pullback. 

With rumors of a Nasdaq-100 inclusion approaching, the stock needs to hold the $147-$150 area and ultimately reclaim $158-$160 to build enough momentum for another breakout.

*

So, don't assume one strong opening erases last week's technical damage. 

The market is attempting a relief rally, but confirmation matters. 

*
